project.name: TrustAnchor
project.domain: Anti-counterfeit certificate issuance and verification web application
confirmedContext: Used by education institutions and government agencies
Combines digital signatures, double encryption, audit logging, and instant verification
Runs on Next.js App Router with TypeScript
assumptionsToValidate: Public verification may safely show the documented public fields
A login gateway is enough for the current admin-facing redesign slice

designEvidenceSummary.currentFrontendShape: Small App Router UI with home, login, verification result, and not-found pages
researchInfluence: Use transform and opacity motion before adding heavy animation dependencies
Preserve accessibility and native browser behavior
Favor small composable UI surfaces and code ownership
Treat motion as useful feedback rather than ornament

newDirection: porcelain, midnight ink, civic blue, oxide, and signal green palette
ruled observatory surfaces with low-radius frames
signal rings, scan sweeps, lane dividers, and registry ticks

designPhilosophy: TrustAnchor should make authenticity observable. The UI treats each certificate as a civic signal that can be checked, locked, and read without exposing private proof internals.

conceptualAnchor.name: Civic Signal Observatory
conceptualAnchor.rationale: The product turns document authenticity into a verifiable public signal. Observatory language supports signal rings, proof lanes, live telemetry, and controlled reading surfaces without falling into a generic dashboard.

derivedTokenLogic.typography: Assertive display text, civic body copy, compact metadata, and monospaced proof strings
derivedTokenLogic.spacing: 8px rhythm with long proof lanes, ruled dividers, and asymmetric desktop composition
derivedTokenLogic.density: Operational medium density with verification-first mobile priority
derivedTokenLogic.colorBehavior: Porcelain and ink for authority, civic blue for action, signal green for validity, amber and oxide for audit and failure
derivedTokenLogic.morphology: Low-radius instruments, framed signal panels, lane dividers, and proof blocks
derivedTokenLogic.motion: Scanner rings, scan sweeps, signal bar pulses, and reveal cascades using CSS transforms and opacity
derivedTokenLogic.responsiveComposition: Mobile verification-first, tablet two-band regrouping, desktop observatory lanes

conceptualAnchor.visualRiskBudget: Expressive but controlled. Signal animations may be visible, but they must not obscure forms, status text, or proof strings.
conceptualAnchor.motionRiskBudget: CSS-only transform/opacity motion for this slice. Stop non-essential animation under reduced motion.
cohesionChecks: Does the first scan make verification feel live and official?
Are proof strings visually distinct from narrative copy?
Do motion effects support signal readiness instead of decoration?
Does mobile promote the verification action before supporting narrative?

visualDirection.name: Civic signal registry
distinctiveMoves: Landing page uses an animated signal field as the primary visual asset
Verification console is visually dominant and dark for focus
Trust chain is rendered as telemetry lanes instead of repeated cards
Report result uses status orbit and proof references instead of a simple success card

mutationRules.mobile: Verification console appears before narrative; signal visualization compresses below the form; proof lanes stack vertically.
mutationRules.tablet: Verification and signal summary form two bands; support sections pair into readable rows.
mutationRules.desktop: Use asymmetric observatory lanes with narrative, verification console, and live signal telemetry visible together.

forbiddenPatterns: desktop order preserved on mobile without justification
equal-weight card rows as the default layout

motionSystem.dependencyDecision: No new animation dependency for this slice
motionSystem.approach: CSS-only transforms, opacity, pseudo-element scan sweeps, and keyframed signal rings

identityRules: Primary actions are solid and authoritative
Instrument surfaces use low-radius frames and ruled dividers
Proof blocks use monospaced alignment and stronger containment than narrative blocks
Status chips include readable text and may not rely on color alone

reviewRubric.distinctiveness: The page should read as TrustAnchor's civic signal registry, not a generic SaaS shell.
reviewRubric.contractFidelity: Signal rings, proof lanes, typography, color, and responsive order must derive from the conceptual anchor.
reviewRubric.hierarchy: Verification must be the most obvious first action.
reviewRubric.responsiveRecomposition: Mobile, tablet, and desktop must change grouping and priority.
reviewRubric.motionDiscipline: Motion must support signal readiness or state feedback and stop under reduced motion.
reviewRubric.accessibility: Focus, contrast, target size, form errors, and status text must remain intact.

forbiddenPatterns: frosted glass as the primary UI language
decorative animation that hides the verification task
proof strings styled like marketing copy
nextValidationAction: Validate the observatory redesign with typecheck, build, responsive inspection, reduced-motion behavior, and the project PR checklist.
